'''Greg Richards''' (born 12 July 1995) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Richards also played in the Super League and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for the [[Leigh Centurions]], the [[London Broncos]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] and [[Toulouse Olympique]].

==Background==
Richards was born in [[Urswick]], [[Cumbria, England]]. He attended [[Ulverston Victoria High School]]

He started his career with amateur club Barrow Island.<ref>{{cite web|title=Rich pickings for Greg in the England squad|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rich-pickings-for-greg-in-the-england-squad-1.1022385|website=North West Evening Mail|publisher=CN Group|accessdate=17 May 2015|date=14 December 2012}}{{dead link|date=February 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

==Career==
===St Helens===
He was signed by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]. He made his first team début in July 2013 in a [[Super League]] game against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

He appeared in the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] as a substitute in Saints' 14–6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Newsum|first1=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: St Helens 14-6 Wigan Warriors|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29559845|website=BBC Sport|access-date=17 May 2015|date=12 October 2014}}</ref>

During the 2017 season, Richards was sent to [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side the [[Sheffield Eagles]] as part of a [[Rugby League Dual registration|Dual registration]] deal with Saints. Here, he played just three games before returning.

===Leigh Centurions===
Shortly after his departure from the Eagles came his departure from the Saints, as he joined then struggling [[Super League]] outfit [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].
[[File:Gregory Richards.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Richards playing for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in 2018]]
He played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[2018 Betfred Championship]].

===London Broncos===
On 31 October 2018 it was announced that Richards had joined the [[London Broncos]] ahead of the [[2019 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/46047828|title=Greg Richards: London Broncos sign ex-Saints forward|work=BBC Sport |accessdate=18 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 8 September 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Sep 2021|accessdate=31 Dec 2021|title=Hull KR snap up Greg Richards|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-snap-up-greg-richards/}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 27 September 2023 it was reported that he would make his stay at [[Toulouse Olympique]] permanent in 2024 on a two-year deal<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Sep 2023|accessdate=28 Sep 2023|title=Hull KR's forgotten man sees future confirmed without announcement from Robins|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-krs-forgotten-man-sees-future-confirmed-without-announcement-from-robins}}</ref>
On 15 October 2023, Richards played in Toulouse Olympique's upset loss in the [[Million Pound Game]] against the [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

On 20 October 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Toulouse Olympique]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Oct 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Former St Helens and Hull KR stars among trio of exits from Toulouse|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-st-helens-and-hull-kr-stars-among-trio-of-exits-from-toulouse}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 16 November 2025, it was reported that he had joined [[Barrow Raiders]] on a one-year deal for the 2026 season..<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Former Hull KR and St Helens star lands deal with Championship big guns for 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-hull-kr-and-st-helens-star-lands-deal-with-championship-big-guns-for-2026}}</ref>
